events.name: TCS InQuizitive 2026
events.details: National-level school quiz competition covering Current Affairs, Science & Technology, AI & Innovation, History, Sports, Environment & Sustainability, Arts & Culture, and General Knowledge. Team participation with a friend.
events.preparation: Complete Expression of Interest Form by 26 Jun 2026

events.name: Lit Sanctum 2026-27 Preliminary Round
events.details: Preliminary round results announced. Finalists have been selected. Final round instructions will be shared with finalists shortly.

events.name: Thanksgiving Day Volunteering Initiative
events.details: Middle school students (like Mihika) are invited to volunteer as mentors to lead 8-10 minute sessions on gratitude for Early Years to Grade 2 students. Sessions will be held during Class Teacher Time (CTT) on 26th November (EY classes) and 27th November (Grade 1-2 classes). Volunteers can team up in pairs and visit one or two classes per day. Students will help young learners understand the importance of gratitude before they deliver handmade greeting cards to support staff on 27th or 28th November.
events.preparation: Prepare to lead a child-friendly session using a simple PPT about gratitude; can invite friends to form pairs

events.name: Green Awareness Campaign Mentorship Initiative
events.details: Middle School volunteers will mentor Early Years and Primary learners in environmental responsibility and sustainability. Includes hands-on sapling/seed planting activity and PPT presentation about environmental protection.
events.preparation: Sessions during CTT; specific dates to be communicated later

events.name: Sugar Awareness Mentorship Initiative
events.details: Middle School volunteers will mentor students on making healthier choices and understanding the impact of excessive sugar consumption on well-being.
events.preparation: Sessions during CTT; specific dates to be communicated later

fyi.summary: Humanities lesson included plotting climate graph on graph paper

TCS InQuizitive 2026 is a prestigious national-level quiz competition open to students. Mihika can participate as part of a two-person team to represent Sancta Maria school.

Math: Learners introduced to Standard form for representing large and small numbers with notebook work
Thanksgiving Day celebration planned for 27th-28th November. Early Years to Grade 2 students are making greeting cards for house-help, drivers, and support staff under art teacher guidance.
Multiple new mentorship initiatives being planned for March for Early Years and Primary learners; additional initiatives being planned and will be communicated later

Middle School Volunteers program wrapping up for the year. Teachers Chaitali and Rozy Ma'am requesting student reflections on community service initiatives throughout the year.

Grade 8 Future Pathways: First Aid For All Initiative - youth-led volunteer opportunity launched by alumna Ms. Aadya Agarwal. Students can register to volunteer and learn life-saving first aid skills. No prior medical background required. Registration form available.

events.name: Battle of the Skits - Lit Sanctum Onstage Event
events.details: Expression of Interest form deadline is 18 Jun 2026 at 10:00 pm
events.preparation: Complete Expression of Interest form or comment interest

events.name: Lit Sanctum's Battle of the Skits - Nominations Deadline
events.details: Preliminary round nominations close at 2:30 p.m.
events.preparation: Student should submit nomination before 2:30 p.m. if participating

events.name: Battle of the Skits Preliminary Round
events.details: Ananya is participating in pairs for Lit Sanctum 2026. Preliminary round auditions were scheduled for 29th and 30th June 2026. Individual learners will be assessed on voice modulation, expression, body language, confidence, creativity, and preparation. Evaluation criteria include: clear message/lesson, voice and expression, body language and movement, teamwork, creativity and humour, preparation and timing.
events.preparation: Ananya should work with her partner during school hours to plan the skit, work on scripts, demonstrate strong voice modulation, expression, body language, confidence, creativity, and preparation.

events.name: Lit Sanctum Week - Author Spotlight Literary Display
events.details: Grade 5 learners will present completed Author Spotlight charts as part of a vibrant literary display celebrating authors. This is not a competition but an inclusive event where every learner's work contributes to the display.
events.preparation: Support child in researching and creating the Author Spotlight chart with required elements: author name and picture, biography, notable works, favourite book explanation, author quote, and interesting facts

fyi.summary: Annual club model adopted for 2026-27. Each club has limited seats, allocated on first-come, first-served basis. Parents encouraged to explore club options with child. Some clubs aligned with ISP programmes. ISP Artist and Chess Masters open to all Primary learners with progression through rounds based on performance.

Audition dates for Battle of the Skits Preliminary Round have been revised to 29th and 30th June 2026. Some learners are grouped into trios where even pairing was not possible. Strongest individual performers from each house will advance to the Grand Finale.
Weekly reading homework will be uploaded every Wednesday under 'Read and Reflect' module in English Teams Classwork tab
Author Spotlight is part of Lit Sanctum 2026 alongside Battle of the Skits. It invites Grade 5 learners to explore books through research, reflection, and creativity to celebrate inspiring authors.

Grade 5D Mathematics: Workbooks introduced for additional home practice. Teachers will assign regular homework in workbooks. Answer keys will be uploaded to Teams after each unit completion for self-reflection. Keep workbooks at home by default unless teacher requests otherwise to reduce school bag weight.
